rationale: The signal set is almost entirely composed of generic search volume (Google autocomplete) and existing video content. While there's strong search demand around "crested gecko morphs," "cappuccino crested gecko," "lilly white crested gecko," and "axanthic crested gecko," the signals lack the forum heat, disagreement, or specific breeding outcomes that would justify a new post. YouTube already has comprehensive coverage on these topics (TikisGeckos alone has multiple morph guides, breeding guides, and cappuccino/lilly white deep-dives). The one queued slug ("super-cappuccino-viability-debate") suggests we're already working the cappuccino space. The autocomplete data shows buyer interest (price, for sale, care) but not breeder-specific confusion or misconceptions. Without evidence of forum discussion, data-backed claims to verify, or a concrete breeding outcome to explain, none of these rise above "general care" or "listicle" territory. The breeder blog signal is promotional (sale announcement), not editorial. I'm rejecting all topics.
